title: Cloud Transformation
lead: Applying ITOM to orchestrate a structured move to cloud — governance, toolchain, sourcing, and process redesign all shaped by ITOM-for-Cloud principles.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Cloud Transformation is one of the most common triggers for redesigning an IT Operating Model. Moving to cloud is not just a technology migration — it fundamentally changes how IT is governed, how services are sourced, how teams are organised, and how costs are managed. An ITOM-for-Cloud addresses these dimensions holistically, ensuring that the operating model evolves alongside the technology landscape rather than lagging behind it.

sections.heading: ITOM Perspective
sections.body: Many organisations adopt cloud technology but retain their legacy operating model — leading to 'cloud chaos': uncontrolled spending, inconsistent security posture, and teams working around governance rather than with it. An ITOM-for-Cloud designs the operating model around cloud-native principles: self-service provisioning, FinOps-driven cost management, platform engineering, and DevOps-enabled delivery. It bridges the gap between cloud strategy (what we want to achieve) and cloud operations (how we run it day-to-day).

items.desc: landing zones, policies, guardrails, and compliance frameworks that enable safe self-service consumption
financial management discipline for cloud — cost visibility, allocation, optimisation, and accountability
internal platforms that abstract cloud complexity and provide golden paths for development teams
roles, processes, and tooling for running cloud at scale — including CCoE (Cloud Centre of Excellence)
the 6 R's (Rehost, Replatform, Refactor, Repurchase, Retain, Retire) and how they shape the transformation roadmap
operating consistently across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private cloud with unified governance and tooling
cloud readiness scan, application portfolio analysis, business case development
target cloud architecture, landing zone design, operating model blueprint, migration waves
wave-based migration execution, automated tooling, testing, and validation
FinOps implementation, performance tuning, modernisation of migrated workloads
steady-state cloud operations, CCoE maturation, continual improvement
Cloud Readiness Scan — assess organisational and technical readiness for cloud adoption
Cloud Strategy & Roadmap — define the cloud adoption path aligned to business objectives
Landing Zone design — implement secure, governed cloud foundations (Azure, AWS, GCP)
Cloud migration services — wave-based migration with automated tooling and validation
FinOps enablement — implement cost management practices and tooling
Managed cloud services — ongoing operations, monitoring, and optimisation
